# Standalone XPU requirements — does NOT inherit requirements_base.txt.
# Packages that are CUDA-only (bitsandbytes, cpm_kernels, pynvml, auto_gptq,
# autoawq, tensorrt*, flash_attn, flashinfer*, deep_gemm, deep_ep, nvidia-*,
# xfastertransformer*, decord, onnx) are deliberately excluded.
#
# Install with:
#   pip install -r deps/requirements_xpu.txt \
#       --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/xpu

# ---------- Pin torch/torchvision to prevent CUDA upgrade ----------
# These must already be installed from the XPU index. Pinning here stops
# pip from pulling the generic (CUDA) torch when resolving transitive deps.
torch==2.12.0+xpu
torchvision==0.27.0+xpu

# ---------- Server framework ----------
fastapi>=0.115.6
uvicorn>=0.30.0
grpcio==1.78.0
grpcio-tools==1.78.0
protobuf>=4.25

# ---------- Model loading ----------
transformers==5.2.0
safetensors>=0.7.0
sentencepiece>=0.2.0
tiktoken>=0.7.0
einops
librosa

# ---------- Core utilities ----------
numpy<2.0a0,>=1.25
pydantic>=2.0
requests
jinja2
orjson
sympy
psutil
pyyaml
filelock>=3.20.0
setproctitle
nest_asyncio
typing-extensions
importlib_metadata
setuptools>=60.5.0

# ---------- Application / API ----------
aiohttp
concurrent_log_handler
dacite
dashscope>=1.11.0
json5
lru-dict
openai
partial_json_parser
Pillow
pillow-avif-plugin>=1.5.2
pillow-heif
portalocker
oss2
prettytable
timm

# ---------- Misc ----------
jieba
sentence-transformers==5.2.0
pybind11_stubgen
py-spy
thrift
# ---------- vllm-xpu-kernels (out-of-band dependency) ----------
# vllm-xpu-kernels provides the FA2 attention kernel that XPU DECODE
# hard-requires. It is NOT on PyPI or the standard PyTorch XPU wheel
# index, so it cannot be pinned in this pip-compile resolution and must
# be installed out-of-band from a custom index or source build.
#
# Contract:
#   - The FA2-enabled build is REQUIRED on XPU. Without it the attention
#     factory fails fast at startup with a RuntimeError (see
#     rtp_llm/models_py/modules/factory/attention/__init__.py); the
#     service will not boot.
#
# Install manually (pin the version matching your torch-xpu build):
#   pip install 'vllm-xpu-kernels==<version>' --find-links <index-url>
